pr.title: Sysdolphin matching, calcprogress, and some of the data/rodata sections moved to assoc. files
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched several SysDolphin baselib routines by introducing C sources/headers for tobj, texp, and memory; moved large blocks of data/rodata out of global asm/data.s and asm/rodata.s into their associated asm files; added a progress-calculation script; and adjusted build flags/object ordering to support the new C matches. There were no PR comments or review notes in the slice.

key_files.reason: New matched C implementation for texture-object animation helpers including HSD_TObjRemoveAnimAll, HSD_TObjAddAnim, HSD_TObjAddAnimAll, HSD_TObjReqAnimAllByFlags, HSD_TObjReqAnim, and HSD_TObjReqAnimAll.
New large TObj header defining TOBJ/TEX constants, HSD_TObj/HSD_TObjDesc/HSD_Tlut/HSD_ImageDesc/HSD_TexAnim structures, and TObj/Tlut/ImageDesc function prototypes.
New matched C implementation for HSD_TExpGetType, HSD_TExpRef, and HSD_TExpUnref.
New TExp enums and struct/union definitions for HSD_TEInput, HSD_TEType, HSD_TExpType, HSD_TETev, HSD_TECnst, and HSD_TExp.
New C wrapper HSD_Free implemented as OSFreeToHeap(HSD_GetHeap(), ptr).
Removed matched TObj routines from assembly and renamed remaining symbols/references such as TObjUpdateFunc and HSD_TlutLoadDesc.
Assembly reduced as TExp functions moved to C, with remaining symbols renamed in related references.
Deleted a large centralized data block, consistent with the PR title's move of data sections to associated files.
Deleted centralized rodata entries that were redistributed into associated asm files.
Added new src/sysdolphin/baselib/tobj.o, memory.o, and texp.o entries before their corresponding asm objects.
Added the Metrowerks C flag -enum int, likely required for enum layout/codegen with the newly introduced headers.
New tool that reads baserom.dol and the generated map file to estimate code/data decompilation progress.
Updated placeholder function names to known HSD names such as HSD_TObjAddAnim, HSD_TlutLoadDesc, HSD_TExpRef, HSD_TExpUnref, HSD_TExpCnst, HSD_GetHeap, and hsdFreeMemPiece.

what_changed: Added matched C source files for SysDolphin baselib: memory.c, texp.c, and tobj.c.
Added matching support headers memory.h, texp.h, and tobj.h with newly documented HSD types, constants, structures, and prototypes.
Removed previously hand-written assembly bodies for several TObj, TExp, and memory routines and built the new C objects instead.
Inserted src/sysdolphin/baselib/tobj.o, memory.o, and texp.o into obj_files.mk while keeping the residual asm objects for still-unmatched code.

Moved large data/rodata blocks out of asm/data.s and asm/rodata.s into associated asm files such as asm/melee/cm/code_80028B9C.s, asm/melee/lb/code_8000E9F0.s, asm/melee/lb/code_80016A54.s, asm/melee/lb/code_8001E2F8.s, lbfile.s, lbsnap.s, lbvector.s, and player-related files.
Renamed a set of unknown func_ symbols to HSD baselib names in include/functions.h and in assembly callsites.
Added tools/calcprogress.py, which parses a DOL header and Metrowerks map-file symbols to compute decompiled code/data byte totals and percentages.
smart_moves: Used paired C-plus-asm object ordering in obj_files.mk so matched C functions could replace the corresponding assembly while unmatched routines remained in the asm object.
Moved data/rodata next to associated code rather than leaving it in global asm/data.s and asm/rodata.s, improving locality and making later file-by-file matching easier.
Introduced detailed headers before or alongside C matches, giving later SysDolphin work reusable structure layouts and named constants instead of raw offsets.
Kept small inline helper variants in tobj.c and texp.c, which is a common matching tactic when Metrowerks inlining decisions affect exact codegen.
Added '-enum int' globally, matching the explicit enum-heavy HSD_TExp/HSD_TObj headers and reducing risk that enum storage/codegen differs from the original compiler output.
Renamed known functions while leaving unknowns as func_ placeholders, avoiding overclaiming names not supported by this diff.
decomp_lessons: When matching a baselib file incrementally, add the new src object before the old asm object and remove only the matched assembly ranges; this allows partial file progress without needing the whole source file complete.
For HSD-style code, accurate struct field offsets are central: tobj.c accesses fields like aobj, imagetbl, tluttbl, tlut_no, id, next, and flags through the new HSD_TObj layout.
Enum sizing can be a build-level matching issue. This PR added '-enum int' at the Makefile level at the same time as adding enum-heavy TExp/TObj headers.
Associated-file data migration is valuable for decomp: function pointer tables, strings, constants, and rodata placed next to their consuming code are easier to identify and later convert to C data.
Sentinel pointer values in decompiled C may need explicit casts: HSD_TExpGetType checks NULL, then compares the pointer value cast to u32 against -1 and -2 for HSD_TE_TEX and HSD_TE_RAS.

Do not assume all tool text is perfectly adapted: calcprogress.py's header says 'Air Ride decomp' while paths and output target this Melee tree, so future agents should treat that as likely copied boilerplate rather than project intent.
naming_conventions: SysDolphin baselib functions use HSD_ prefixes, e.g. HSD_TObjAddAnim, HSD_TObjReqAnimAll, HSD_TlutLoadDesc, HSD_TExpRef, HSD_TExpUnref, HSD_Free, and HSD_GetHeap.
HSD structures follow typedef names like HSD_TObj, HSD_TObjDesc, HSD_Tlut, HSD_TexAnim, HSD_TExp, HSD_TETev, and HSD_TECnst, often backed by tagged structs/unions named _HSD_*.
Texture-object macros use TOBJ_ and TEX_ prefixes, with helper macros like HSD_TOBJ(o), HSD_TOBJ_INFO(i), HSD_TOBJ_METHOD(o), tobj_coord(T), tobj_colormap(T), and tobj_alphamap(T).
Texture-expression enum constants use HSD_TE_* and HSD_TExpType values such as HSD_TE_ZERO, HSD_TE_TEV, HSD_TE_TEX, HSD_TE_RAS, and HSD_TE_CNST.
Unknown symbols were not all force-renamed; remaining unidentified functions stayed as func_ addresses, while better-known symbols were promoted to HSD names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted matched assembly functions from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/tobj.s and asm/sysdolphin/baselib/texp.s while keeping the rest of each assembly file intact.
Used source object insertion before asm object insertion in obj_files.mk for tobj.o, memory.o, and texp.o to support partial replacement.
Added inline helper functions such as HSD_TExpGetType_inline, HSD_TObjRemoveAnim, HSD_TObjReqAnimByFlags, and HSD_TObjReqAnimAllByFlags_inline to influence inlining/codegen.
Used exact null-check and loop structure patterns in TObj list walkers to match linked-list assembly behavior.
Used explicit TRUE comparisons and early returns in HSD_TExpRef/HSD_TExpUnref, preserving branch structure visible in the original assembly.
Renamed assembly labels/calls where names became known, for example lbl_8035E860 to TObjUpdateFunc and func_8035EDC0 to HSD_TlutLoadDesc.
Moved .section .data and .section .rodata blocks into associated asm files, reducing reliance on global asm/data.s and asm/rodata.s.

review_feedback.detail: The PR slice reports 0 issue comments, 0 review comments, and 0 reviews; no human feedback examples were available.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue matching src/sysdolphin/baselib/tobj.c or replace more of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/tobj.s.
Find later PRs that revise HSD_TObj or HSD_TExp struct layouts introduced in pr-128.
Find later PRs that update or replace tools/calcprogress.py after the initial Air Ride boilerplate text.
Search for additional PRs moving global asm/data.s or asm/rodata.s blocks into associated asm/source files.
Search for build-system PRs discussing '-enum int' and Metrowerks enum sizing.
